title: Underlying Conditions and the Higher Risk for Severe COVID-19 | Covid | CDC
text:

Prescribe antivirals, which have been shown to significantly decrease the risk of hospitalization and death when treating patients with mild or moderate illness and risk factors for severe illness. Outcomes are improved if therapeutics are started within the first 5-7 days of symptom onset. 
Consider Pemivibart (Pemgarda™), a monoclonal antibody for COVID-19 pre-exposure prophylaxis in people who are moderately or severely immunocompromised and unlikely to mount an adequate immune response to COVID-19 vaccination and who meet the FDA-authorized conditions for use . Pemivibart is an IV-infusion monoclonal antibody that is authorized for pre-exposure prevention of COVID-19 for individuals (12 years of age and older weighing at least 40 kg). Pemivibart may provide another layer of protection against COVID-19 in addition to vaccination and can be given at least 2 weeks after receiving a COVID-19 vaccine. Healthcare providers should consult the Pemivibart EUA Fact Sheet and EUA Frequently Asked Questions for the FDA-authorized conditions under which Pemivibart may be used.
